data.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 33d0 "Collapse the dual outbound applier paths to one production path"), cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/59ef/822a/8937/fe57) and corroborated against the repo. The 7 cross-child interaction modes were each checked; ONE high-confidence finding (a cluster of interacting modes between 33d0 and sibling 2359 over the SAME files / WHICH-PATH-SURVIVES assumption). The symmetric counterpart is already filed on 2359; recorded here for this pairing's record from 33d0's side. Remaining modes read benign (detailed below).

---
FINDING — implicit shared state + conflicting assumptions + ordering violation + consumer impact (33d0 ↔ 2359)
- finding: 33d0 (collapse the dual outbound applier paths) and sibling 2359 (surface outbound sub-op telemetry + silent-no-op canary) both rewrite the SAME outbound-apply region of the SAME files with no sequencing between them, and 33d0's "which path survives" decision is left explicitly open in a way that can invalidate 2359's central deliverable. 33d0 What: "Either delete the dead typed leaf `_apply_outbound_update` (and its now-misleading tests), OR make it the single production path and route the batch dispatch through it." 2359 plumbs `links_applied`/`comments_applied`/`labels_applied` counters + the `computed>0 && applied==0` canary into the BATCH path (`batch_dispatch.py` `update_one`, `applier.py` `RECON: batch_outcome` line) and cites "parity with the typed leaf's existing `links_applied`/no-op guard" as its reference model. If 33d0 chooses option 2 (route batch through the typed leaf) — or deletes the typed leaf whose `links_applied`/no-op guard 2359 references — 2359's instrumentation either lands on a non-surviving path or loses its cited reference model. Neither ticket declares a `depends_on`; both merely list the other as "Out", which partitions WORK but not the SHARED FILE STATE (batch_dispatch.py / applier.py) or the surviving-path assumption.
  - criteria: 33d0 AC1 (exactly one applier path; retired path deleted), 33d0 AC2 (combined multi-sub-op UPDATE through the production path); parent AC3/SC2 (33d0) vs parent AC4/SC3 (2359)
  - location: 33d0 What/Scope vs 2359 What/Scope; src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/apply_outbound.py (_apply_outbound_update counters + no-op guard, L244-400: labels_applied list L245/L389, comments_applied int L278, links_applied int L325, no-op skip-guard L372-385); batch_dispatch.py (update_one link/comment/label dispatch, L490-561 — NO per-sub-op counter, label/comment/link failures print to stderr and silently continue); applier.py (RECON batch_outcome L816-821 emits only action/key/error); reconcile.py L75 ((outbound,update) → applier._apply_outbound_update) vs batch_dispatch.py L387-388 comment ("the production outbound dispatch routes through this legacy batch path, NOT the typed leaf")
  - evidence: 33d0 file_impact = apply_outbound.py ("retire/consolidate typed leaf") + batch_dispatch.py ("production outbound path") + applier.py ("routing"); 2359 Scope.In = "outcome/telemetry plumbing in batch_dispatch.py / applier.py / reconcile.py"; repo: batch path (batch_dispatch.py update_one) has NO links_applied counter and logs sub-op failures only to stderr, while the typed leaf apply_outbound.py DOES carry links_applied/comments_applied/labels_applied + the no-op guard 2359 re-derives; the codebase already contains BOTH a typed-leaf dispatch route (reconcile.py L75 / typed_dispatch.py L56) and a batch route (batch_dispatch.update_one) — the exact dual path 33d0 collapses
  - scenarios: (a) 33d0 lands option 2 (batch routed through the typed leaf) → 2359's batch_dispatch counter/canary code is bypassed or removed; the silent-no-op canary that "would have caught the link drop" no longer guards the live path → parent AC4/SC3 silently regress to green-on-the-live-path, reinstating the bug-3f04 class on whichever path the counter is not carried into. (b) 33d0 deletes the typed leaf (option 1) → 2359 loses the `links_applied`/no-op-guard "parity" model it cites, and any test 2359 added against the typed leaf becomes a residual reference. (c) either order → same-region merge conflict in the ~40-line link/comment/label dispatch block.
  - impact: parent AC4/SC3 ("a silent sub-op no-op fails a test/canary instead of passing green") and parent AC3/SC2 (one production path) can both be checked off individually yet leave the surviving path UN-instrumented; avoidable same-region merge conflict; a residual reference to the deleted typed leaf in 2359.
  - checklist_item: cross-child modes — implicit shared state / conflicting assumptions / ordering violation / consumer impact
  - suggested_fix: sequence 33d0 BEFORE 2359 (collapse to ONE path first, then instrument the single survivor) and add an explicit `2359 depends_on 33d0`; OR state the canonical surviving production path in BOTH tickets so 2359 targets it unconditionally; OR fold the counter/canary requirement into 33d0's "single path" AC so the surviving applier carries telemetry by construction (and the labels-as-list vs count normalization is resolved on the one survivor).

---
Modes that read BENIGN (not filed):
- Scope overlap: 33d0 AC2 ("combined multi-sub-op UPDATE asserts each sub-op took effect") vs 822a AC1 (bidirectional comment/link/parent ROUND-TRIP) vs 2359 AC2 (no-op CANARY) are distinct intents (consolidation-correctness vs inbound↔outbound round-trip vs computed>0&&applied==0 invariant), exercising the same production path from different angles — complementary, not duplicative; 33d0 declares telemetry OUT (→2359) consistently.
- Dependency gap (on inputs): 33d0 is pure code consolidation needing no schema/fixture input, so — unlike siblings 8937/59ef/822a which correctly declare depends_on fe57 — its lack of an input dep is appropriate. (The 33d0↔2359 sequencing gap is captured in the finding above under ordering.)
- Consumer impact on the contract test: 8937 AC3 / 822a AC1 drive the differs→applier through "the production path"; once 33d0 collapses to one path their tests target the survivor by construction — benign provided 33d0 lands first, which the suggested sequencing already addresses.
- Residual references / conflicting assumptions with 8937/fe57/59ef: none — those stories key on the inbound snapshot/fetch surface, not the outbound applier 33d0 rewrites.

The roster-level parent-AC6 absence (documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNING new tests) is a G3 coverage matter already tracked across the sibling set, not a G4 cross-child inconsistency, so not re-filed here.
